text.content: Build a developer sub-agent capability for AInchors. Not a single dev agent — a framework for spinning up, training, and cloning coding agents as a team. Key design goals: (1) Containerised — each dev agent runs in an isolated container. Code changes, dependencies, and side effects are sandboxed. No risk to production platform. Links to TKT-0045 (containerised OpenClaw architecture). (2) Trained — agents are pre-loaded with AInchors codebase context, conventions, RULES.md, relevant skill files, and project-specific instructions. Not generic coding agents — trained on our stack. (3) Cloneable — a base dev agent image that can be cloned per task/project. Spin up as many as needed, dispose after task. (4) Team delivery framework — orchestration layer (Yoda as lead) that assigns tasks to dev agents, tracks progress, reviews output, and merges. Modelled on a human dev team: lead + specialists. (5) Model exploration — evaluate qwen3.x (coding variant), Qwen2.5-Coder, deepseek-v3-coder, or other high-performance coding models. Target: best code quality at lowest cost, ideally local on OC2 for data sovereignty. Deliverables: (a) Model evaluation report (T2 research), (b) Base dev agent design (SOUL.md + RULES.md), (c) Container spec, (d) Team orchestration workflow, (e) First task end-to-end proof of concept.
